title: Quamba2 A Robust and Scalable Post-training Quantization Framework for Selective State Space Models
abstract: Despite State Space Models (SSMs) are emerging as an efficient alternative to Transformers, depolying SSMs on both cloud and edge devices is challenging due to the limited resources. Model quantization reduces model size and leverages hardware acceleration, and recent efforts on SSM quantization have focused on optimizing a particular model or bit-width. However, distinct bitwidths are essential for different scenarios, like W4A8 for boosting large-batch decoding speed, and W4A16 for enhancing generation speed in short-prompt single-user applications. We present Quamba2, compatible with W8A8, W4A8, and W4A16 for both Mamba1 and Mamba2 backbones, addressing the growing demand for SSM deployment. Based on channel order preserving and activation persistence of SSMs, we propose an offline approach to quantize inputs of the linear recurrence in 8-bit by sorting and clustering for input x, combined with a per-state-group quantization for input-dependent parameters B and C. To ensure compute-invariance in the SSM output, we rearrange weights offline according to the clustering sequence. We show that Quamba2-8B outperforms two state-of-the-art SSM quantization methods and delivers 1.3× and 3× speed-ups in the pre-filling and generation stages, respectively, while offering 4× memory reduction with only a 1.6% average accuracy drop. The evaluation on MMLU shows the generalizability and robustness of our framework. The code and quantized models are released at the link.
impact: This paper aims to enhance the efficiency of machine learning and expand the accessibility of large language models. We find that the accuracy degradation is not negligible. Despite this, the performance trade-off is acceptable given the significant improvements in latency and resource efficiency. Our work enables large language models to be deployed on resource-limited devices. As a positive feature, our method may push the development of privacy-centric on-device applications, where sensitive data can be processed locally without relying on cloud services. However, our work may also present challenges such as increased device resource usage and potential security vulnerabilities if the local devices are compromised.

query_input.scenario: W8A8 SSM inference for cloud NLP services with compute-invariant optimization
query_input.issue: Channel-order-preserving quantization could introduce hidden biases in sequence modeling, altering text generation subtly.
hazard: Post-training quantization of SSM weights and activations induces per-state-group approximation distortion, degrading model fidelity under low-bit regimes.
exposure: End-users of cloud-based SSM text generation APIs

hipporag_result.vulnerability: End-users have no means to detect or audit subtle quantization-induced distortions, relying implicitly on API output fidelity.
hipporag_result.impact: Undetected biased or hallucinated text corrupts downstream decisions, spreads misinformation, and erodes user trust in automated services.
hipporag_result.dose_response: Per-state-group quantization distortion in SSM inference, exposed through cloud APIs to end-users, triggers their inability to detect subtle fidelity loss, leading to biased text corrupting downstream decisions and eroding trust.

dose_response: Post-training quantization of SSM weights and activations induces per-state-group approximation distortion, which in W8A8 SSM inference for cloud NLP services exposes end-users to biased text, leading to uncritical acceptance and amplification of inequalities.
vulnerability: Users uncritically accepting AI-generated text as neutral and accurate without bias detection tools.
impact: Widespread dissemination of subtly biased content amplifying existing societal inequalities in downstream applications.
